##NAMES=Tintinaite
##RRUFFID=R110163
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Pb_10_Cu_2_Sb_16_S_35_
##LOCALITY=Tintina Silver mine, Watson Lake, Yukon Territory, Canada
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Big piece from Royal Ontario Museum (M37320) and two smaller pieces from Mark Feinglos. 
##DESCRIPTION=Cut slab of malachite-stained weathered quartz containing massive lead-grey veinlets. Co-type material.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R110163
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 34.001 b: 22.311 c: 4.0335 alpha: 90 beta: 90.013 gamma: 90 volume: 3059.8 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
